name: Abraysham Kabaub (Silk Kebab)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve sugar in water over medium heat until fully dissolved.
2. Bring to a boil, then add lemon juice and saffron (if using). Continue boiling for 10 minutes before straining into a pie plate. Set aside.
3. In a casserole dish about 20 cm (8 inches) in diameter, beat eggs with salt until yolks and whites are well combined but not foamy.
4. Heat oil to 190°C (375°F). Prepare a skewer, syrup plate, baking sheet, and nuts mixed with cardamom. Keep water and cloth nearby for cleaning hands.
5. Holding the dish of eggs in one hand, dip your palm down into it so that the back of your hand is coated. Quickly lift and place fingers down onto hot oil, forming strands. Repeat until about an eighth of the egg has been used, creating a loosely woven layer across 20 cm (8 inches).
6. Rinse hands quickly and dry them.
7. Using a skewer, slide under the omelet to flip it over and lightly brown the other side. The first side will be bubbly with a smoother underside.
8. When golden brown, lift out with the skewer and place on a baking sheet.
9. Pour syrup over the top of the omelet and roll up so that the crispy side is inside. The final roll should be about 3 cm (1 ¼ inches) in diameter.
10. Repeat steps 5-9 for remaining eggs, creating 7 to 8 rolls total.
11. Once cool, cut into 4-5 cm (1 ½ - 2 inch) pieces and serve. Store in a sealed container in a cool place.
